creator: rainer@ultra-secure.de
text:
On the main-page, it says:

"The PHP development team announces the immediate availability of PHP 7.0.31. This is a security release. Several security bugs have been fixed in this release. All PHP 7.0 users are encouraged to upgrade to this version."


They don't mention any CVEs, so I guess technically you are correct.
;-)

However, according to http://php.net/supported-versions.php PHP 7.0 has not had "Active Support" since 2017-12-03 and will receive only security updates until the end of December this year.
Which to me means that any update to PHP 7.0 is a technically a security update.

Anyway, my life does not depend on PHP 7.0.31 being in 2018Q3 - it would just be nice ;-)
